input.background: Consider a high-voltage load-sharing system consisting of a power supply and two transmitters, say A and B, using mechanically tuned magnetrons. Two transmitters are used, each tuning one-half the desired frequency range; however, if one transmitter fails, the other can tune the entire range with a resultant change in the expected time to failure. For this system to work, the power supply and at least one of the transmitters must work. The transmitters operate independently when both are functional. An undetected fault in either of the transmitters causes the system to fail. Transmitter A has constant failure rate λ_A if transmitter B is operating and failure rate λ_A' if transmitter B has failed. Similarly, transmitter B has constant failure rate λ_B if transmitter A is operating and failure rate λ_B' if transmitter A has failed. λ_P and λ_C are the constant failure rates of the power supply and fault coverage, respectively.
input.question: Obtain the mean time to failure (MTTF) of the high-voltage load-sharing system with dependent failures.

output.answer: The system MTTF is given as follows: $$ \operatorname{MTTF} = \frac{1}{λ_A+λ_B+λ_P} + \frac{λ_A}{(λ_A+λ_B+λ_C-λ_B')} \left(\frac{1}{λ_P+λ_B'} - \frac{1}{λ_A+λ_B+λ_C+λ_P}\right) + \frac{λ_B}{(λ_A+λ_B+λ_C-λ_A')} \left(\frac{1}{λ_P+λ_A'} - \frac{1}{λ_A+λ_B+λ_C+λ_P}\right) $$
